headline_summary: Reuters (Apr 9): ADNOC CEO says Strait of Hormuz is shut and must reopen unconditionally; UAE output hit by effective closure.
Reuters (Apr 8-9): Hormuz traffic near standstill; Iran warns ships to keep to its waters/permit rules, sustaining shipping disruption despite ceasefire talk.
Reuters (Apr 8): Shippers and insurers seek clarity before resuming transits; normalisation could take weeks even if restrictions ease.
Al Jazeera (Apr 7): Russia and China block a UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz, complicating diplomatic pressure on Iran.
NBC News (Apr 9): Shipping traffic through Hormuz remains effectively at a standstill despite ceasefire signals, highlighting ongoing chokepoint risk.
Reuters (Apr 5): OPEC+ debates/considers output policy tied to Hormuz reopening, underscoring oil-supply sensitivity to maritime access.
risk_drivers: Effective Strait of Hormuz closure / permit-based passage and coercive enforcement risk
High likelihood of renewed incidents given warnings to vessels and near-standstill traffic
Diplomatic blockage at UN (Russia/China) reduces near-term multilateral leverage
Oil supply/freight insurance sensitivity to chokepoint normalization pace

Energy: oil_up_risk: Prolonged or selectively enforced Hormuz restrictions keep upside skew in crude and products; physical loadings may queue.
War-risk premia: Tanker rates and war-risk insurance likely stay elevated until routine, unthreatened transits resume.
Inventory timing risk: Delayed sailings can create regional dislocations (Asia/Europe) even if headline production is steady.
FX: em_fx_pressure: Higher energy import bills pressure oil-importer EM FX; Gulf FX pegs stay stable but liquidity conditions can tighten.
risk_off_flows: Escalation/chokepoint headlines typically support USD/CHF/JPY vs high-beta FX.
Rates: rates_credit_spreads: Risk-off and inflation uncertainty can widen credit spreads and lift inflation breakevens.
Policy reaction: Central banks may have to balance growth risks vs energy-driven inflation persistence.
Equities: risk_off_flows: Transport/airlines and energy-intensive sectors face margin risk; energy and defense can outperform on shock headlines.
Regional equities volatility: GCC/Iraq equities can swing between oil-price benefit and operational/security tail risks.

sources.title: Strait of Hormuz is shut, must reopen without conditions, UAE oil giant ADNOC's CEO says
Hormuz at near standstill as Iran warns ships to keep to its waters
Strait of Hormuz shipping traffic is effectively at a standstill despite Iran ceasefire
Russia and China block UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz
OPEC+ agrees to boost oil output when Strait of Hormuz reopens

events.title: ADNOC CEO says Strait of Hormuz is shut and must reopen unconditionally; warns of market tightening
events.implication: Reinforces that physical flows remain constrained; supports sustained oil price risk premium and elevated tanker/insurance costs.

events.title: Hormuz traffic near standstill as Iran warns ships to keep to its waters
events.implication: Near-standstill conditions imply continuing delays and rerouting; keeps risk of sudden supply squeezes and freight spikes.

events.title: Shipping traffic through Hormuz remains effectively at a standstill despite ceasefire signals
events.implication: Even with de-escalation headlines, the chokepoint is not normalising—prolonging war-risk premia and energy volatility.

events.title: Russia and China block UN resolution on Strait of Hormuz
events.implication: Reduces probability of fast UN-backed pressure on Iran; increases odds shipping disruption persists absent bilateral deals.

events.title: OPEC+ debates output policy linked to Hormuz reopening
events.implication: Signals producers are conditioning supply decisions on shipping access; amplifies oil price sensitivity to reopening news.

events.title: Ceasefire implementation uncertainty keeps insurers and shippers cautious on Gulf transits
events.implication: Slow resumption keeps pressure on freight and supply chains; prolongs risk-off episodes on escalation headlines.
